title: HELP REQUEST Good distributer of Limited Edition Blu Rays
display_title: HELP REQUEST Good distributer of Limited Edition Blu Rays
wikitext:
[]: HELP REQUEST: Good distributer of Limited Edition Blu-Rays             Quote Post by Sparrowhawk » Thu Sep 18, 2014 1:59 am Hello All, So I have grown very tired of researching this and of the trial and error of ordering a copy of 1.11 and 2.22 that displays a picture of the LE release only to receive a standard 1 disc blu-ray. You would not believe how many I have returned at this point. I simply want a Region A limited edition blu-ray with English and Japanese audio as well as english subtitles. Ideally, I would like a reliable seller for both 1.11 and 2.22. In case I am not clear. I seek the blu-rays that were released in the simple black text on solid color cases. I felt that this forum would be my best chance of getting some help. Sorry if this is not the appropriate place; feel free to relocate. Thanks.

[]:             Quote Post by Nuclear Lunchbox » Thu Sep 18, 2014 2:08 am Just do a series of Google searches. It took me all of five seconds to find 2.22. 1.11 might be harder, but you just need to look for it.

[]:             Quote Post by Sparrowhawk » Thu Sep 18, 2014 2:13 am See, it seems that way. But I have ordered 3 copies of 2.22 via 3 different distributers and none were the limited edition. Each of them used the picture of the LE case. 2 even said 2 disk in the descriptionbut were not. So ultimately, I am seeking someone that can confirm a seller that indeed has the LE.

[]:             Quote Post by Nuclear Lunchbox » Thu Sep 18, 2014 2:14 am Do you know who originally distributed the items in the first place?

[]:             Quote Post by Sparrowhawk » Thu Sep 18, 2014 2:24 am Yes, but because there were limited releases they cannot be ordered from them anymore. Therein lies the problem. Im prepared to pay more to attain them. Im just tired of doing so and then having to fight with the return policy of the seller when the shit turns out to be a standard blu-ray. It's been a frustrating journey. I really just want to one day have the limited realeases of all four. But I am about to give up.

[]:             Quote Post by Jäeger » Thu Sep 18, 2014 4:26 am I cannot believe people in USA have so much difficulties to purchase Evangelion. in Spain is as easy as buying Dragon Ball dvds

[]: Re: HELP REQUEST: Good distributer of Limited Edition Blu-Ra             Quote Post by TheFriskyIan » Thu Sep 18, 2014 7:52 am Sparrowhawk wrote: I simply want a Region A limited edition blu-ray with English and Japanese audio as well as english subtitles. Ideally, I would like a reliable seller for both 1.11 and 2.22. In case I am not clear. I seek the blu-rays that were released in the simple black text on solid color cases.

[]:             Quote Post by FreakyFilmFan4ever » Thu Sep 18, 2014 8:23 am Limited Edition DVDs and Blu-rays of Evangelion only existed in Japan, and only for a limited time. (Hence the term used.) They are distributed by King Records, IICR, and the included items that made them special edition (film strips from the movie itself and/or soundtracks) have long since been discontinued. Only the video discs themselves remain in current Japanese releases. Ian's right, none of the Japanese releases for Eva have English audio or subtitles included on the disc. If you want Limited Edition Eva releases specifically, there might be some people selling their used copy on eBay. But you can’t find them new anymore, and you’ve never been able to find them for sale by an official distributor in countries outside of Japan.

[]:             Quote Post by pwhodges » Thu Sep 18, 2014 8:39 am YesAsia describes a H-K release of Q as "Limited Edition", but doesn't use the term for earlier films. No English audio, of course, but subtitles. The limited edition comes with the booklet - there is a cheaper one that doesn't. Funimation released 2.22 in a plain cover, but changed it to a picture fairly soon. I don't think the contents of the package changed, which is probably why the sellers don't care which cover they're giving you. Again, YesAsia advertises the US version in a solid colour cover - you could try them, I guess. Like FFF, I can't find any hint of a version of 1.11 in a solid colour with English; I'm sure Funimation's initial release had a picture, as did 1.01 before it. Do you try asking the sellers for confirmation of the cover art before purchasing?

[]:             Quote Post by Nuclear Lunchbox » Thu Sep 18, 2014 9:43 am Yeah, I couldn't find any solid color English 1.11. I'm guessing that the other members are right and that it was a Japanese-only release.

[]:             Quote Post by FreakyFilmFan4ever » Thu Sep 18, 2014 3:09 pm pwhodges wrote: Funimation released 2.22 in a plain cover, but changed it to a picture fairly soon. I don't think the contents of the package changed, which is probably why the sellers don't care which cover they're giving you. Again, YesAsia advertises the US version in a solid colour cover - you could try them, I guess.

You would not believe how many I have returned at this point. I simply want a Region A limited edition blu-ray
